name: Chruscik (Polish Bows)
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg yolks with the confectioners' sugar until well combined.
2. Add sour cream, rum, and vanilla to the egg yolk mixture and mix until smooth.
3. Sift together the flour, baking powder, and salt, then add them to the egg yolk mixture gradually. Knead the dough on a heavily floured surface vigorously, punching and squeezing as much flour into it as possible until the dough is no longer sticky (approximately 1/2 hour).
4. Divide the dough into several portions and roll very thin. Turn the dough often while rolling to prevent sticking.
5. The dough should look like parchment paper that you can see through. Cut the dough into strips about 1 1/2 inches wide and 4 inches long.
6. Make a slit closer to one end of each strip, then bring the longer end through the slit.
7. Heat oil to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and fry quickly (only a few seconds) until golden, not brown. Turn only once.
8. Drain on paper towels and dust with confectioners' sugar. Makes 8 dozen.
